name: BOTSv3 - Frothly Incident Campaign (August 20, 2018)
description: Combined MITRE ATT&CK mapping for three parallel incidents from BOTSv3 dataset: IR-2018-001 (EC2 IMDS Credential Theft), IR-2018-002 (S3 Misconfiguration + Cryptominer), IR-2026-0701-001 (Multi-Region RunInstances Sweep). All incidents occurred on 2018-08-20. Author: Insiyah Bhatia

techniques.comment: IR-2018-001: Compromised web_admin IAM credentials used directly via stolen IMDS credentials; IR-2026-0701-001: Compromised web_admin credentials used directly (no AssumeRole)
IR-2018-001: 11.7MB malicious payload delivered to mars.i-08e52f8b5a034012d from 34.215.24.225:9998
IR-2018-001: Non-standard port 9998 used for C2 beaconing to 34.215.24.225; continued 4+ hours post-containment
IR-2018-001: Malware queried IMDS at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/EC2InstanceRole (HTTP 200) — IMDSv1 in use

IR-2018-001: IAM enumeration burst (6 API calls in <1 sec: GetCallerIdentity, GetSessionToken, ListAccessKeys, CreateAccessKey, DeleteAccessKey, CreateUser); IR-2026-0701-001: Multi-region RunInstances sweep doubles as capability mapping
IR-2018-001: CreateUser (my_db_user) attempted — AccessDenied by IAM policy
IR-2018-001: CreateAccessKey attempted — AccessDenied by IAM policy

IR-2018-001: 576 RunInstances attempts across 15 regions (0 successful); IR-2018-002: Coinhive Monero miner executing in browsers via WebSocket; IR-2026-0701-001: Multi-region RunInstances sweep across ≥10 regions for cryptomining/botnet
IR-2018-001: 2.9MB inbound / 59KB outbound at 18:24:34 — possible second stage or exfiltration over C2
IR-2018-002: Coinhive JS delivered via brewertalk.com browsing session in Chrome (drive-by compromise)
IR-2018-002: Attacker uploaded Coinhive miner payload to exposed frothlywebcode S3 bucket
IR-2018-002: S3 bucket frothlywebcode publicly writable for 56 minutes via accidental PutBucketAcl by bstoll
IR-2026-0701-001: ListAccessKeys on web_admin for credential enumeration
IR-2026-0701-001: Multi-region RunInstances sweep (recon + action combined) across ≥10 regions
IR-2026-0701-001: Root cause hypothesized as credentials in files (phishing/leak); no conclusive evidence

legendItems.label: IR-2018-001: EC2 IMDS Credential Theft & Cloud Abuse
